title: enums with #[non_exhaustive] but no serde catch-all will fail on unknown wire values
description: Trigger: server sends any unknown string for ChatKind, ChatMemberRole, DeliveryState, OwnerPresence, or ChannelPermissionTargetType (e.g. a future 'thread' ChatKind, 'owner' role, 'bounced' DeliveryState, 'dnd' OwnerPresence). Bad behavior: entire parent object deserialization fails with serde error, crashing the update cycle. These enums have #[non_exhaustive] which prevents exhaustive match in Rust but does nothing for serde — only #[serde(other)] on a unit variant handles unknown wire values. ContactPresence correctly has Unknown catch-all; the others do not. Fix: add #[serde(other)] Unknown/Other unit variant to each enum, consistent with ContactPresence pattern.
close_reason: Added #[serde(other)] Unknown catch-all to ChatKind, ChatMemberRole, DeliveryState, OwnerPresence, ChannelPermissionTargetType. Added tests for each. 84 unit tests passing.
